key_insight: Human and LLM collaboration achieved comparable recipe-generation success, and the closed-loop process helped discover a new material prototype, Ba3PtO5, in the Ruddlesden-Popper-related phase space.
engineering_relevance: This work shows that pairing LLMs with experimental feedback can accelerate synthesis planning for materials discovery, especially when exploring underrepresented structure types.
method: Generate synthesis recipes for known and candidate new materials using humans and LLMs
Test recipe success through in-lab experimentation
Feed results back into a closed-loop collaboration and repeat across rounds
evidence_signal: Across two rounds, success rates were similar for humans and LLMs on known materials but much lower for unknown materials, with the reported discovery of Ba3PtO5 validating the approach.
watch_item: Performance drops sharply for unknown targets, so experimental throughput and error control remain critical when translating LLM-generated recipes to the lab.
paper_title: Human and LLM Collaboration for Accelerated Materials Synthesis and Discovery
